pattern: Always drawing from the current largest value and putting back one more than what was taken is optimal at every step, so the whole process collapses to a fixed run of consecutive values starting at the original maximum.
hints: At every step, taking the largest value currently available is never worse than taking anything else.
After a value is taken, it comes back one bigger than before, so the next largest available value is always exactly one more than the last one taken.
This means the k values added are just k consecutive integers starting at the original maximum, so their sum has a closed form.

edge_inputs.description: four-way tie at the maximum value
k equal to one, must just return the max untouched

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(100, size % 100 + 1))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [nums, k]
